sourceMovie.title: Elite Squad
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Elite Squad for the same reason it broke through in 2007: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. José Padilha keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Training Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Elite Squad clicked for you, Training Day (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Substitute
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Elite Squad clicked for you, The Substitute (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Elite Squad?
faq.answer: Try Training Day, The Substitute, The Principal—each shares DNA with Elite Squad while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Elite Squad?
faq.answer: Elite Squad (2007) is best described as Drama, Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Elite Squad part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Elite Squad Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
